verbGlossary.SEGMENTS: {source} subdivides {target} for comparison across project types (e.g., project type segments staffing ratio to compare healthcare vs. commercial)
verbGlossary.SCALES: {source} proportionally increases the demand reflected in {target} or role (e.g., total value band scales the staffing ratio, number of stories scales superintendent span of control)
verbGlossary.DETERMINES: {target} sets the required level of {source} staffing ratio (e.g., peak workforce determines safety staffing ratio per OSHA guidelines)
verbGlossary.REQUIRES: {source} attribute makes the target role mandatory on {target} (e.g., BIM requirement requires BIM manager, LEED certification requires commissioning agent)
verbGlossary.ACTIVATES: {source} delivery or scope attribute creates the need for {target} (e.g., design-build delivery method activates design manager, specialty systems activate project engineer)
verbGlossary.INTENSIFIES: {source} condition attribute increases the staffing level for {target} beyond baseline (e.g., fast-track intensifies scheduler workload, occupied-during intensifies safety manager presence)
verbGlossary.DRIVES: {source} dimension attribute increases {target} workload metric (e.g., gross building area drives PM workload index)
verbGlossary.DEMANDS: {source} complexity attribute creates a staffing need for {target} (e.g., number of subcontractors demands procurement manager, phased construction demands assistant superintendent)
verbGlossary.STAFFS: {source} self-perform attribute determines the deployment of {target} (e.g., self-performed work staffs foreman and journeyman craft workers)
verbGlossary.SHAPES: {source} delivery or owner attribute influences the scope and authority of {target} (e.g., delivery method shapes preconstruction manager responsibilities, owner type shapes owner's representative authority)
